title: cristaria aspera var. sadae
intro: Cristaria aspera var. sadae, also known as the Chinese water chestnut or water caltrop, is a type of aquatic plant native to East Asia, characterized by its unique, rounded or angular seed pods and edible tubers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cristaria aspera var. sadae include lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing the risk of certain cancers, as well as its potential to aid in the management of diabetes and obesity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its tubers are also consumed raw or cooked as a vegetable.
